career_history.title: Plant Manager, Battery Module Pre-Assembly
career_history.scope: Leads one battery module pre-assembly operation with 280 employees, 6 direct reports, output/quality/delivery accountability, and close works council interface

key_achievements.achievement: Stabilised output during a period of supplier-driven component variability
key_achievements.metric: Output attainment improved from 82% to 96% over 6 months; premium freight spend reduced 19%

key_achievements.achievement: Improved first-pass quality through tighter containment and escalation discipline
key_achievements.metric: Internal defect rate reduced 27%; customer-line disruptions reduced from 6 incidents/quarter to 1

career_history.title: Operations Manager — E-Drive Components
career_history.scope: Managed one e-drive component manufacturing area with 110 employees; shift operations, quality, and productivity

key_achievements.achievement: Led productivity improvement initiative during line transfer phase
key_achievements.metric: OEE improved from 76% to 86% over 12 months

career_history.title: Manufacturing Engineer / Team Lead
career_history.scope: Process engineering and early team leadership in powertrain-related production

key_achievements.achievement: Improved station design and rework discipline in a high-variability production area

leadership_indicators.leadership_style_self_described: Clear, dependable, and people-focused without losing accountability. I believe operational discipline and trust have to reinforce each other.
leadership_indicators.decision_making_style: Balanced and pragmatic. Moves quickly on known issues but prefers stakeholder alignment on changes that affect workforce stability or quality risk.
leadership_indicators.conflict_handling: Constructive and calm. Known for de-escalating tension with works council and quality teams without appearing weak.
leadership_indicators.change_orientation: Moderate; supportive of improvement and digitization when grounded in operational reality.
leadership_indicators.risk_appetite: Measured; unlikely to make reckless bets.

notable_leadership_moments.situation: Battery-module output dropped due to incoming component inconsistency and shift-level blame cycles.
notable_leadership_moments.action: Weber imposed one-point accountability for containment, added daily supplier-quality reviews, and involved works council early when shift patterns needed short-term adjustment.
notable_leadership_moments.outcome: Output recovered without labor escalation and quality incidents fell materially.

performance_reviews.summary: Seen as highly reliable, credible with workforce stakeholders, and increasingly strong on quality and operational leadership. Development need is broader strategic presence.

interview_notes.overall_impression: A very plausible future BMW production leader and one of the safer internal options in the younger cohort. Strong on people, quality, and operational stability. Less distinctive on transformation or large-network strategic acceleration.

question_responses.question: How would you respond to a talent-poaching wave in production engineering?
question_responses.response_summary: Focused on manager quality, visible career paths, and targeted retention for critical skills rather than broad financial reactions.

interview_notes.case_study_performance: Solid and realistic. Good plant logic, moderate ambition, limited boldness.

red_flags: Scope still too narrow for full EMEA Head of Production credibility
Not a natural crisis or transformation spike candidate
Could be outgunned by stronger external operators in high-pressure scenarios
green_flags: Good works council and people-management credibility
Quality and operational stability profile fits BMW culture well
Low interpersonal risk with Klaus, Thomas, and Hans-Peter
